results.label: Hangang National University - Emily Johnson
results.summary: The Certificate of Admission is authentic in structure, well-formatted, contains all required fields, valid dates, and official signatures/seals with no indicators of fraud.

documentStructure.note: The document contains a clear letterhead, organized body content, a signature block with contact details, and professional visual layout.

programPeriodValidity.note: The program period starts on September 1, 2024, and ends on June 30, 2025, which is chronologically logical.

dateConsistency.note: All dates (issue date, orientation date, and program duration) are correctly and consistently formatted, with no unrealistic future dates.

documentFormatting.note: Consistent typography, clean borders, professional iconography, and proper alignment are observed throughout the document.

officialSealSignature.note: Includes both the signature of the Director of the International Office and the Hangang National University seal.

requiredFields.note: Student Name, ID, program dates, department, and official contact email (international@hnu.ac.kr) are all clearly listed.

noFraudIndicators.note: No signs of tampering, impossible dates, joke credentials, or spelling errors in critical fields were identified.

results.label: Donggang International University - Lucas Müller
results.summary: The exchange student acceptance letter meets all structural, formatting, and date consistency requirements with no indicators of fraud.

documentStructure.note: The document contains a clear letterhead, reference number, metadata block, body, signature block, institutional seal, and footer.

programPeriodValidity.note: The program duration runs from March 2, 2025, to December 19, 2025, which is chronologically logical.

dateConsistency.note: The issue date is September 20, 2024, which precedes the program start date in March 2025 and is correctly formatted.

documentFormatting.note: High-quality document with consistent font usage, proper alignment, professional table borders, and clear layout.

officialSealSignature.note: Contains both a realistic signature from 'Prof. Park Soo-yeon' and the Donggang International University institutional seal.

requiredFields.note: All necessary fields including student name, ID, home university, program dates, and hosting department are present.

noFraudIndicators.note: No spelling errors, impossible dates, joke credentials, or unprofessional formatting elements were detected.

results.label: Admission Letter - James Kim (Suspicious)
results.summary: The document is invalid due to multiple critical fraud indicators, including impossible dates, an illogical program period, joke academic credentials, and highly suspicious email/seal details.

documentStructure.note: The letter follows a basic standard structure containing a letterhead, body text, table of student details, signature, and footer contact block.

programPeriodValidity.note: The program end date (2022-12-31) is before the program start date (2023-01-01).

dateConsistency.note: The issue date '2026-15-45' is completely invalid, utilizing an impossible month (15) and day (45), and is set in the future.

documentFormatting.note: The document contains obvious spelling errors ('aprooved', 'Congratlation!') and highly unprofessional phrasing ('maybe in spam folder', 'No need to contact office').

officialSealSignature.note: A signature and seal are present, but the seal contains highly suspicious/unprofessional text: 'CERTIFIED AUTHENTIC REAL UNIVERSITY'.

requiredFields.note: Key fields such as student name, ID, program dates, and contact information are physically present.

noFraudIndicators.note: Multiple critical fraud indicators detected: impossible dates, logical chronological errors in dates, an absurd scholarship amount (999,999,999 KRW), joke credentials ('Ph,D,D,D,D'), and a fake email TLD ('.fake').

issues: Program end date (2022-12-31) is before the start date (2023-01-01).
The issue date '2026-15-45' contains invalid month (15) and day (45) values.
The email address 'info@kieuniv.fake' uses an invalid top-level domain (.fake).
Spelling errors in key official text: 'aprooved' instead of 'approved' and 'Congratlation!' instead of 'Congratulations!'.
Joke academic credentials listed for the signatory: 'Ph,D,D,D,D,MBA'.
Highly unprofessional language ('maybe in spam folder' and 'No need to contact office').
The official seal contains mocking text: 'CERTIFIED AUTHENTIC REAL UNIVERSITY'.
